delivered.fact: Tardigrades are the only animals known to survive direct exposure to the vacuum of space. In 2007, European scientists sent them aboard a satellite, and after 10 days in open space, many survived rehydration. They've also survived temperatures from -272°C to 150°C and radiation doses 1,000x lethal to humans.

Honey never spoils. Archaeologists found 3000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible. Its low moisture content, acidic pH, and natural hydrogen peroxide create an inhospitable environment for bacteria and microorganisms.

Octopuses have three hearts. Two pump blood to the gills, one pumps to the organs. The organ heart stops when they swim, so they prefer crawling; it's less exhausting. Their blood is also blue due to copper-based hemocyanin.
